title: Iranian Military Shows It Knows How to Adapt, U.S. Officials Say
summary: Iran appears to be targeting what it views as American vulnerabilities, including air defenses meant to guard troops and assets in the region.

title: How Long Will the Iran War Last? Trump Offers Conflicting Answers.
summary: Now 11 days into an expanding military campaign, President Trump and his officials have given conflicting indications on how long the United States intends the war to last.

title: Senate Moves Toward Passing Sweeping Housing Bill, but Challenges Lie Ahead
summary: The legislation’s progress is all the more surprising because it addresses an issue that is shaping up to be the main battleground of the midterm elections: affordability. The effort could still stall.

title: Judge Is Skeptical of Penn’s Argument Against Trump Demand for List of Jews
summary: A federal judge appeared receptive to the Trump administration’s demands for information about the University of Pennsylvania’s Jewish faculty and staff as the government pursues an antisemitism investigation.

title: Kennedy to Undergo Rotator Cuff Surgery
summary: The secretary of health and human services, known for his Make America Healthy Again campaign, is expected to be back at work on Monday.

title: Clayton Fuller and Shawn Harris Advance to Runoff in Marjorie Taylor Greene’s Georgia District
summary: Clayton Fuller will face Shawn Harris, a Democrat, in an April runoff. Mr. Fuller’s win over a flashier Republican showed the power of the president’s support.

title: Can a Populist Democrat Flip New York City’s Only G.O.P. House Seat?
summary: Allison Ziogas, a first-time candidate, hopes to unseat Representative Nicole Malliotakis by appealing to the district’s union roots.

title: Culture at Columbia Shielded Sexual Assault by Physician, Report Finds
summary: Two doctors affiliated with the university and NewYork-Presbyterian Hospital stepped down after an external report found that the administrators mishandled allegations of abuse against Robert Hadden.

title: San Francisco Bay Area Residents Weigh Possibility of BART Reductions
summary: The Bay Area Rapid Transit system was once so successful, it could rely mostly on riders to sustain itself. But the pandemic dealt BART an unusually heavy blow.

title: Noma Loses American Express and Blackbird Sponsors For Los Angeles Dinners
summary: Two companies have withdrawn from a series of dinners in Los Angeles after The New York Times reported allegations that René Redzepi attacked workers.

title: Michelle Pfeiffer Makes It Look Easy. It Isn’t.
summary: At 67, the actress is still reinventing herself, with two new TV shows, “The Madison” and “Margo’s Got Money Troubles,” and an evolving attitude about her work.

title: A.I. Incites a New Wave of Grieving Parents Fighting for Online Safety
summary: Blaming chatbots, they are joining an earlier push for better protections by parents who say social media contributed to their children’s deaths.

title: Mother of Tumbler Ridge Shooting Victim Sues OpenAI
summary: The company banned the shooter’s ChatGPT account but did not alert the authorities, a move that amounted to fatal negligence, the family claims.

title: ChatGPT, Other Chatbots Approved for Official Use in the Senate
summary: New guidelines said Senate aides could use A.I. tools for official work, including research, drafting and editing documents, and preparing briefings and talking points for lawmakers.

title: Meta Acquires Moltbook, the Social Network Just for A.I. Bots
summary: Matt Schlicht, the site’s creator, helped kick off Silicon Valley’s obsession with artificial intelligence agents. Two months later, he is joining the Meta Superintelligence Lab.

title: Who Are the Men Accused of Bringing Homemade Bombs to Gracie Mansion?
summary: A high school senior and a recent graduate, both from the Philadelphia suburbs, were charged by federal prosecutors with trying to support a terrorist group.

title: Gov. Kay Ivey Commutes Death Sentence of Charles ‘Sonny’ Burton
summary: It is only the second commutation for Gov. Kay Ivey, a Republican. The inmate, Charles Burton, was scheduled to die even though the gunman’s sentence had been commuted years ago.

title: Review: A New ‘Tristan und Isolde’ at the Metropolitan Opera
summary: Yuval Sharon’s debut production featured an astonishing performance by the soprano Lise Davidsen. It is the event of the season.

title: How Does This End? Four Scenarios for What Comes Next With Iran.
summary: The best hope? Weaken the regime’s ability to lash out and resist an uprising.

title: Trump’s Smash-and-Grab Presidency Reaches New Heights
summary: When disruption becomes destruction.

title: The U.S. Showers Iran With Bombs
summary: Also, Alabama spares a man on death row. Here’s the latest at the end of Tuesday.

title: How Do You Use A.I. for Daily Life?
summary: We want to hear how artificial intelligence helps you with tasks like meal prep, translation and planning a trip.

title: Texas Woman Found Innocent After 22 Years in Prison Faces Deportation Threat
summary: Carmen Mejia was cleared of murder this week in connection with the death of a baby in her care, who was scalded by hot water in 2003 in Austin, Texas. She is still in jail on an immigration hold.

title: 2 Former Police Officers Conspired to Steal From Prostitute, U.S. Says
summary: The two men pleaded not guilty on Tuesday in federal court to charges that they violated a woman’s civil rights while responding to a complaint about a brothel in a Queens building.

title: Matt Snell Dies at 84; Carried Jets to Stunning Upset in Super Bowl III
summary: His pounding runs for the underdog New York team against the Baltimore Colts secured a pivotal win for the American Football League.

title: Bill Kurtis to Leave NPR’s ‘Wait Wait … Don’t Tell Me!’
summary: As the irreverent quiz show’s judge and scorekeeper, Kurtis, a former news anchor known for his rich voice, has helped find comedy in current events since 2014.
